title: Hooks, Quality Gates, Signing, and Repository Security · Graph Lens: Hook Portability
summary: Add useful local feedback without pretending client-side hooks enforce server policy or executing untrusted repository code silently. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git hook portability increment in a bounded Git automation and trust policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Keep hook logic fast, deterministic, documented, opt-in where appropriate, and delegated to versioned scripts.
Map client and server hook names to operations, inputs, environment, bypass behavior, and failure effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Hook Portability
steps.instruction:
theory case — A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Hook Portability contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for keep hook logic fast, deterministic, documented, opt-in where appropriate, and delegated to versioned scripts.

content.text: A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Hook Portability condition decided your prediction.

content.value: githooks Manual — Controls client and server hook invocation, environment, parameters, bypass, and exit behavior.

options.text: Keep hook logic fast, deterministic, documented, opt-in where appropriate, and delegated to versioned scripts. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
A hook that works in one shell is a portable team gate. This does not resolve predict hook portability.

For predict hook portability, apply Hook Portability without its stated preconditions, omit the a bounded Git automation and trust policy trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Hook Portability for a bounded Git automation and trust policy.

content.text: Keep hook logic fast, deterministic, documented, opt-in where appropriate, and delegated to versioned scripts. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Hook Portability while producing a bounded Git automation and trust policy.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust. Competency probe: Keep hook logic fast, deterministic, documented, opt-in where appropriate, and delegated to versioned scripts. A defensible solution applies Hook Portability, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a bounded Git automation and trust policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-hook-portability — Keep hook logic fast, deterministic, documented, opt-in where appropriate, and delegated to versioned scripts.
# Evidence: changed Git case 3
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A hook that works in one shell is a portable team gate. This fails the Hook Portability contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
